solution: ```
class Solution:
    def maximumOr(self, nums: List[int], k: int) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        prefix = [0] * n
        suffix = [0] * n
        
        prefix[0] = 0
        for i in range(1, n):
            prefix[i] = prefix[i-1] | nums[i-1]
        
        suffix[-1] = 0
        for i in range(n-2, -1, -1):
            suffix[i] = suffix[i+1] | nums[i+1]
        
        max_or = 0
        for i in range(n):
            candidate = prefix[i] | (nums[i] << k) | suffix[i]
            if candidate > max_or:
                max_or = candidate
        return max_or
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Compute prefix and suffix OR arrays to isolate the effect of doubling any single element k times. The optimal strategy is to apply all k operations to one element; the prefix and suffix arrays allow O(1) evaluation of the OR result for each candidate element, yielding the maximum possible OR in linear time.
